free_text: uncertainties of data from various experiments.
The data for total (g,Sn) and partial (g,1n), (g,2n)
and (g,3n) reactions obtained in two experiments
carried out at Livermore (USA) were analyzed.
The new approach to analysis of systematic
disagreements between data was proposed on the base
of new criteria of systematic uncertainties
Using these functions calculated in the frame of
combined photonuclear reaction model new reliable
and free from problems of neutron multiplicity
sorting data for photoneutron partial reactions
were evaluated using experimental neutron yield
Data were compiled at the Russia MSU SINP
VV+SD: DOI of EPJ/A,53,180 was corrected,
The total neutron reaction cross section obtained  as
The contribution  of  partial  reaction (g,1n)  cross
section  into  the  experimental total neutron yield
For the better agreement  between  the  experimental
neutron  yield  cross  section and the correspondent
theoretical one the latter was  shifted in  0.15 MeV
up to  larger  energies  and multiplied by 0.90.
Experimental  data  for   the  total  neutron  yield
reactions based on the Fermi gas densities.
The contribution  of  partial  reaction  (g,2n) cross
section  into  the  experimental total neutron yield
For the better agreement  between  the  experimental
neutron  yield  cross  section and the correspondent
theoretical one the latter was  shifted in  0.15 MeV
up to  larger  energies  and multiplied by 0.90.
Experimental  data  for   the  total  neutron  yield
reactions based on the Fermi gas densities.
The contribution  of  partial  reaction  (g,3n) cross
section  into  the  experimental total neutron yield
For the better agreement  between  the  experimental
neutron  yield  cross  section and the correspondent
theoretical one the latter was  shifted in  0.15 MeV
up to  larger  energies  and multiplied by 0.90.
Experimental  data  for   the  total  neutron  yield
reactions based on the Fermi gas densities.
